title: The Prompting Milestone
concept: PHASE D6 COMPLETE. We have mastered prompt design, context management, RAG integration, and prompt security. Boss explain ends with: 'Phase D6: Prompt Engineering & Context is complete. Next up: Efficient LLM Inference.'

exercises.prompt: Which major AI phase have we been mastering to guide LLMs effectively?
exercises.explain: Prompt engineering is the foundation of steering LLMs, but to build complete systems, we also need to understand how to serve models efficiently.

cards.title: The Bridge to Mastery
cards.body: You've learned how to talk to AIs, feed them facts, manage their memory, and protect them from hackers. Now, it's time to celebrate this milestone and see how all these pieces fit together to build ultimate AI systems!

cards.title: What is the Prompting Milestone?
cards.body: The Prompting Milestone marks the completion of our prompt engineering journey. It brings together prompt design, context window management, Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG), and prompt security (like stopping injection attacks) into a single, cohesive system that guides AI safely and accurately.

cards.title: The Director's Cut
cards.body: Think of prompt engineering like directing a movie. A bad director just says, 'Act sad.' A great director gives the actor a backstory (context), script examples (few-shot), safety rules (guardrails), and clear stage directions. Both get a performance, but only the director with great instructions gets an Oscar-worthy scene!

cards.title: Putting It All Together
cards.body: A robust AI system doesn't just ask an LLM questions. It checks the user input for prompt injection, retrieves relevant facts from a vector database (RAG), fits them within the context window limits, applies a strict system prompt, and outputs clean JSON.

cards.title: Myth: Just Nice Words
cards.body: Many believe that 'prompt engineering' is just about writing polite or clever sentences. In reality, professional prompt engineering is a system engineering discipline involving security, database retrieval (RAG), token limits, and structured outputs.

cards.title: Basic vs. Production Prompting
cards.body: Let's compare a fragile, basic prompt with a professional, production-ready prompt system.

left.text: Just asking a direct question and hoping the AI doesn't hallucinate or get hacked.

right.text: RAG context + Guardrails + Sanitized user input + System instructions.

exercises.prompt: Why is prompt security (defending against prompt injection) crucial for public AI applications?

options.text: To make the model run faster.
To prevent malicious inputs from hijacking the AI's core instructions.
To compress the size of the prompt.
To translate prompts into multiple languages automatically.

exercises.explain: Prompt injection is a safety threat where malicious users try to hijack the AI's instructions. A secure system must filter and neutralize these attempts.

exercises.prompt: True or False: RAG systems improve accuracy by retrieving external data and inserting it into the prompt.
exercises.explain: RAG stands for Retrieval-Augmented Generation. It actively pulls relevant data from external sources to ground the model's responses.

exercises.prompt: Fill in the missing word to complete the core principle of prompt management.
exercises.sentence: To help an LLM give accurate answers, we must carefully manage its context window.
exercises.explain: Context is the background information we feed the LLM. Managing it carefully ensures the model gets the right facts without overflowing its limits.

exercises.prompt: Which of these represents a complete, professional prompt engineering pipeline?

options.text: An AI assistant that directly runs user text inside system prompts without validation.
An AI assistant that validates inputs, uses RAG for facts, and enforces strict output formats.
A static chatbot that only replies with pre-written templates and does not use an LLM.

exercises.explain: The prompt engineering system (Option B) is structured and secure, whereas Option A is vulnerable to injections and hallucinations.

exercises.prompt: As a master prompt engineer, what is the ultimate approach to building robust, production-grade LLM applications?

options.text: Creating a single, massive prompt with no safety boundaries or structure.
Combining clear instructions, dynamic context, safety guardrails, and structured outputs.
Relying entirely on the LLM to guess the context without providing any external data.
Using prompt injection to bypass safety filters for faster response times.

exercises.explain: Fantastic job! You've successfully navigated the challenges of designing, securing, and optimizing prompts for LLMs. Phase D6: Prompt Engineering & Context is complete. Next up: Efficient LLM Inference.
